findings.title: Unauthenticated access to sensitive endpoint: /api/users
findings.description: The endpoint '/api/users' returned data without requiring authentication.
findings.evidence: GET /api/users → HTTP 200, Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8, Body: [{"id":16,"name":"Test User","email":"test@example.com","password":"password123","role":"USER","created_at":"2026-03-20T07:49:44.37+00:00"},{"id":15,"name":"Naveenkumar","email":"naveenkumar.vtab@gmai
findings.recommendation: Protect ALL API endpoints with authentication middleware. Return HTTP 401 for unauthenticated requests.

findings.title: Potential Google API Key exposed in JavaScript bundle
findings.description: A pattern matching a Google API Key was found in the JavaScript bundle. Hardcoded secrets in frontend code are visible to anyone who views the page source.
findings.evidence: File: https://analytic-core.netlify.app/assets/index-Dh3k4TCU.js | Pattern: Google API Key | Match: AIzaSyCFBcb0cqc8BHHG8fLXY1d_Dlx4QnldiUQ
findings.recommendation: Never embed secrets in frontend code. Move all secrets to backend environment variables. Rotate any exposed keys immediately. Use secret scanning in your CI/CD pipeline.

findings.title: Missing header: Content-Security-Policy
findings.description: Missing CSP header — increased risk of XSS and data injection.
findings.evidence: Header 'Content-Security-Policy' not present in response from https://analytic-core.netlify.app
findings.recommendation: Define a strict CSP. At minimum: default-src 'self'

findings.title: No account lockout or rate limiting on login
findings.description: Made 6 consecutive failed login attempts without triggering lockout or rate limiting. An attacker can brute-force passwords.
findings.recommendation: Implement account lockout (e.g., lock after 5 failures for 15 min) or exponential back-off + CAPTCHA. Use a WAF rule on the login endpoint.

findings.title: CORS: untrusted origin 'http://localhost:3000' reflected with credentials
findings.description: The API reflects the untrusted origin 'http://localhost:3000' in ACAO and allows credentials. An attacker-controlled site can make authenticated API requests on behalf of logged-in users.
findings.evidence: Origin: http://localhost:3000 → ACAO: http://localhost:3000, ACAC: true
findings.recommendation: Maintain an strict allowlist of trusted origins. Reject or drop the ACAO header for unknown origins.

findings.title: Unauthenticated access to sensitive endpoint: /api/health
findings.description: The endpoint '/api/health' returned data without requiring authentication.
findings.evidence: GET /api/health → HTTP 200, Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8, Body: {"status":"ok","version":"1.0.3-dashboard-overwrite-fix","uptime":639719.556879922,"timestamp":"2026-04-10T15:10:27.882Z"}
findings.recommendation: Protect ALL API endpoints with authentication middleware. Return HTTP 401 for unauthenticated requests.

findings.title: Missing header: X-Content-Type-Options
findings.description: Missing X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff — browser MIME sniffing enabled.
findings.evidence: Header 'X-Content-Type-Options' not present in response from https://analytic-core.netlify.app

findings.title: Missing header: X-Frame-Options
findings.description: Missing X-Frame-Options — app may be embeddable (clickjacking risk).
findings.evidence: Header 'X-Frame-Options' not present in response from https://analytic-core.netlify.app
findings.recommendation: Add: X-Frame-Options: DENY  (or use CSP frame-ancestors directive)

findings.title: No rate limiting on API endpoint
findings.description: Made 30 rapid requests to /api/search without receiving HTTP 429. Without rate limiting, the API is vulnerable to automated abuse, scraping, and DoS.
findings.recommendation: Implement API rate limiting (e.g., 60 requests/minute per IP or user). Use Netlify Edge functions or a CDN WAF rule for this. Return HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header when the limit is exceeded.

findings.title: Missing header: Referrer-Policy
findings.description: Missing Referrer-Policy — sensitive URL data may leak to third parties.
findings.evidence: Header 'Referrer-Policy' not present in response from https://analytic-core.netlify.app

findings.title: Missing header: Permissions-Policy
findings.description: Missing Permissions-Policy — browser features (camera, mic, etc.) unrestricted.
findings.evidence: Header 'Permissions-Policy' not present in response from https://analytic-core.netlify.app

findings.title: Information leak via header: Server
findings.description: Reveals server software version — remove or anonymize.
findings.recommendation: Remove or anonymize the 'Server' response header.
